description: End-to-end evaluation scenario: 5 controls + 8 policy chunks. Tests the full pipeline from indexing through meta-requirement resolution.

controls.title: Access Enforcement
controls.description: Enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.

controls.title: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
controls.description: Enforce a limit of consecutive invalid logon attempts by a user and automatically lock the account when the maximum number of unsuccessful attempts is exceeded.

controls.title: Protection of Information at Rest
controls.description: Protect the confidentiality of information at rest.

controls.title: Content of Audit Records
controls.description: Ensure that audit records contain information that establishes the following: What type of event occurred; When the event occurred; Where the event occurred; Source of the event; Outcome of the event; and Identity of any individuals, subjects, or objects/entities associated with the event.

controls.title: System Backup
controls.description: Conduct backups of user-level information and system-level information. Protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of backup information.

chunks.raw_text: The system enforces role-based access control (RBAC) for all logical access to information resources. Users are granted access based on their assigned roles, and access permissions are validated at each point of entry using centralized authentication services.

After five consecutive failed login attempts, the user account is automatically locked for 30 minutes. The security team is notified of repeated lockouts, and persistent failed attempts trigger an investigation by the incident response team.

All sensitive data stored on servers, databases, and endpoint devices is encrypted at rest using AES-256 encryption. Full disk encryption is enabled on all laptops and mobile devices. Encryption keys are managed centrally through the key management system.

Each audit record includes the timestamp, source IP address, user identity, event type, event outcome (success or failure), and the affected resource. Records are stored in a standardized format compatible with the SIEM platform.

Full system backups are performed weekly and incremental backups are performed nightly. Backup media is encrypted using AES-256 and stored at a geographically separate offsite facility. Backup restoration is tested quarterly to verify data integrity.

This policy has been approved by the Chief Information Security Officer on January 15, 2024. It supersedes all previous versions and is effective immediately upon publication. All employees must acknowledge receipt.

All employees complete annual security awareness training covering phishing identification, password hygiene, social engineering tactics, and data handling procedures. Role-based training is provided for IT staff.

Media protection policies define handling requirements for digital and non-digital media containing sensitive information. Media is classified according to the data it contains and protected with appropriate physical safeguards.
